evaluation.test_case: Phase 10 Verification Step 1 — running automated checks

phase_10_step_1.objective: Run the test suite, linter, type-checker, and build commands
phase_10_step_1.primary_responsibility: Execute all four automated checks and record results

execution_model.parallelism_rule: test-suite steps may run in parallel subagents if the test framework supports it

parallel_subagent_conditions.condition_1: Test framework explicitly supports parallel execution
parallel_subagent_conditions.condition_2: Commands are independent (no shared state conflicts)
parallel_subagent_conditions.condition_3: Linter and type-checker can run concurrently

strategy_A_sequential.name: Sequential (Default)
steps: Run npm test → record exit code
Run npm run lint → record exit code
Run npm run typecheck → record exit code
Run npm run build → record exit code

strategy_C_full_parallel.name: Full Parallelism (Rare)
strategy_C_full_parallel.when: Test framework and all commands support concurrent execution

standard_report_template.field_1: Command: npm test | npm run lint | npm run typecheck | npm run build
standard_report_template.field_5: Error summary: (first 500 chars of stderr if exit code != 0)
standard_report_template.field_9: Test results: (for npm test: N tests, N passed, N failed)

example_npm_lint_fail.error_3: tests/http.test.ts:105: 'x' is defined but never used

subagent_must_report: Failed test names and stack traces (if failures)

npm_run_lint.exit_code_0_means: No linting errors (warnings may be allowed)

conditions: No High severity findings remain from Phase 9

failure_handling.if_any_check_fails: Step 5: Return to Phase 8 to fix
failure_handling.steps_2_3_continue: For diagnosis even if Step 1 fails
failure_handling.verification_issue: Created as open status with FAIL indicators

evaluation_result.finding: Phase 10 Step 1 must run all four commands (npm test, npm run lint, npm run typecheck, npm run build) from test-commands.yaml or inferred sources. Execution is sequential by default in the primary agent, but can launch parallel subagents if the test framework explicitly supports parallelization. Each subagent must report: command, exit code, status (PASS/FAIL), output, and failure details. All four commands must exit with code 0 to pass Gate F.
evaluation_result.rationale: The specification is explicit in workflow.md Phase 10, with parallelism rule clearly stated. The report requirements are implied by the verification template and GitHub artifact schema.
